description: Boot power-on sequence adapted from M1 reference (Eason @home_cpu_fix) for M2. Sequence starts from USB-C cable insertion and ends at display LOGO.
reference: schematics/reference/M1 series standby circuit and power-on principle, and 20V boost circuit.pdf

sequence.name: USB-C Cable Insertion — 5V Default
sequence.action: USB-C cable inserted. Charger provides default 5V on VBUS.
sequence.what_happens: CD3217 internal MOSFET passes 5V. Output renamed PPVL_APCL_ACMON.
sequence.next_trigger: CD3217 detects 5V on VBUS → begins CC communication

sequence.name: PPCON_ACNSW — Charger Input Active
sequence.action: Charger input path enabled. PPCON_ACNSW feeds U5200 charger IC area.
sequence.what_happens: Charger IC begins regulating input → generates PPBUS_AON from adapter or battery

sequence.name: PPBUS_AON Established
sequence.action: Main power bus comes up. If battery connected, PPBUS_AON ≈ 12.6V. If adapter only, follows adapter voltage.
sequence.prerequisite: BMU SYS_DETECT circuit (J5150 gasket) must enable VBAT output

sequence.name: PP3V8_AON — SVR AON Powers Up
sequence.action: U5700 converts PPBUS_AON → PP3V8_AON (3.8V, 30A max). This is the master rail for both PMUs.
sequence.delay: 3.8V VR enable delayed by D5340/D5345 circuit (page 26)
sequence.next_trigger: PP3V8_AON present → SPMU (U7700) and MPMU (U8100) begin startup

sequence.name: SPMU & MPMU Startup — PMU Rails Cascade
sequence.action: Both PMUs receive PP3V8_AON and begin generating output rails in hardware-defined sequence.
sequence.next_trigger: All S2/S1 rails stable → PMU generates enable for standalone VRs

sequence.name: UC300 (5V) & UC710 (3V3) Power Up
sequence.action: Standalone buck converters generate PP5V_S2 and PP3V3_S2 from PPBUS_AON, enabled by PMU GPIO signals.
sequence.next_trigger: PP5V_S2 + PP3V3_S2 present → load switches enabled → subsystems powered

sequence.name: SoC AWAKE Rails — DVFS Domains Power Up
sequence.action: SoC dynamic voltage domains enabled. PCPU, ECPU, GPU, SRAM come up at initial DVFS voltage.
sequence.next_trigger: All SoC domains stable → M2 OTP ready → hard start complete

sequence.name: M2 Hard Start Complete — SoC Commands CD3217
sequence.action: M2 SoC has completed OTP verification and hard start. SoC sends command to CD3217 via internal bus.
sequence.what_happens: CD3217 reads ROM information from SPI ROM

sequence.name: PD Negotiation — 20V Boost
sequence.action: CD3217 uses CC line to communicate PD Source_Capabilities with charger. Requests 20V.
what_happens: CD3217 sends PD request via CC line

sequence.name: Protection Isolation Passes — PPBUS_AON Stabilises
sequence.action: With 20V on VBUS, protection isolation circuit passes. PPBUS_AON stabilises at battery voltage (12.6V) with charger now providing full power.

sequence.name: Soft Start — SSD, BIOS, Display
sequence.action: M2 SoC communicates with SSD (NAND0), reads BIOS/firmware, executes self-checking procedures, outputs display signals.
what_happens: SoC reads SSD via NAND interface (pages 71-72)
Display power sequencer (UP700/UP705) enables panel rails
Display PMU (U6600) generates PP1V05_DISP, PP_AVDD_DISP, PP_VGH_DISP, PP_VGL_DISP

bypass_forced_start.description: From M1 reference PDF: How to bypass protective isolation and forced start of 20V boost circuit
bypass_forced_start.method_1: If charger IC cannot be started, check if it cannot be connected. Use a 9V jumper or connect to charger for 3 seconds.
bypass_forced_start.method_2: Can be forced to start → indicates fault range is at the front and board can proceed to diagnostics
bypass_forced_start.method_3: Can't force start → indicates fault range is at the back end (soldering conditions)
